title: Land grid array (lga) packaging of passive-on-glass (pog) structure
abstract: A device includes a passive-on-glass (POG) structure and an interface layer. The POG structure includes a passive component and at least one contact pad on a first surface of a glass substrate. The interface layer has a second surface on the first surface of the glass substrate such that the passive component and the at least one contact pad are located between the first surface of the glass substrate and the interface layer. The interface layer includes at least one land grid array (LGA) pad formed on a third surface of the interface layer, where the third surface of the interface layer is opposite the second surface of the interface layer. The interface layer also includes at least one via formed in the interface layer configured to electrically connect the at least one contact pad with the at least one LGA pad.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A device comprising: a passive-on-glass (POG) structure comprising: a glass substrate; a passive component on a first surface of the glass substrate; and at least one contact pad on the first surface of the glass substrate; and an interface layer comprising a second surface on the first surface of the glass substrate such that the passive component and the at least one contact pad are located between the first surface of the glass substrate and the interface layer, wherein the interface layer comprises: at least one land grid array (LGA) pad on a third surface of the interface layer, wherein the third surface of the interface layer is opposite the second surface of the interface layer; and at least one via in the interface layer configured to electrically connect the at least one contact pad with the at least one LGA pad. 2. The device of claim 1 further comprising a printed circuit board (PCB), wherein the PCB is coupled to the at least one LGA pad on the third surface of the interface layer. 3. The device of claim 2, wherein the PCB comprises a ground plane, and wherein the passive component is separated from the ground plane by a distance that is independent of a line spacing utilized by the PCB. 4. The device of claim 3, wherein a value of the passive component is dependent on the distance separating the passive component and the ground plane. 5. The device of claim 1, wherein the passive component comprises a capacitor. 6. The device of claim 1, wherein the passive component comprises an inductor. 7. The device of claim 1, wherein the interface layer is a mold formed over the passive component and the at least one contact. 8. The device of claim 7, wherein the mold comprises a low-loss insulating material. 9. A package, comprising: a passive-on-glass (POG) structure comprising: a glass substrate; a passive component on a first surface of the glass substrate; and at least one contact pad formed on the first surface of the glass substrate; a mold comprising a second surface on the first surface of the glass substrate and on the passive component and the at least one contact pad, such that the passive component and the at least one contact pad are located between the first surface of the glass substrate and the mold, wherein the mold comprises: at least one land grid array (LGA) pad on a third surface of the mold, wherein the third surface of the mold is opposite the second surface of the mold; and at least one via formed in the mold and configured to electrically connect the at least one contact pad with the at least one LGA pad; and a printed circuit board (PCB) comprising: a ground plane; and a top metal layer comprising a PCB contact pad, wherein the PCB contact pad is directly connected to the at least one LGA pad on the third surface of the mold. 10. The package of claim 9, wherein the passive component is separated from the ground plane by a distance that is independent of a line spacing utilized by the PCB. 11. The package of claim 10, wherein a value of the passive component is dependent on the distance separating the passive component and the ground plane. 12. The package of claim 9, wherein the passive component comprises a capacitor. 13. The package of claim 9, wherein the passive component comprises an inductor. 14. A device, comprising: a passive-on-glass (POG) structure comprising: a glass substrate; a passive component on a first surface of the glass substrate; and at least one contact pad on the first surface of the glass substrate; at least one land grid array (LGA) pad on a surface of the device that is to be incident with a printed circuit board (PCB) of a package; and means for electrically connecting the at least one contact pad with the at least one LGA pad while maintaining a distance between the passive component and a ground plane of the PCB that is independent of a line spacing utilized by the PCB. 15. The device of claim 14, wherein a value of the passive component is dependent on the distance between the passive component and the ground plane. 16. The device of claim 14, wherein the passive component comprises a capacitor. 17. The device of claim 14, wherein the passive component comprises an inductor. 18. A method of forming a device, the method comprising: providing a passive-on-glass (POG) structure comprising: providing a glass substrate; disposing a passive component on a first surface of the glass substrate; and forming at least one contact pad on the first surface of the glass substrate; and forming an interface layer having a second surface on the first surface of the glass substrate such that the passive component and the at least one contact pad are located between the first surface of the glass substrate and the interface layer, wherein forming the interface layer further comprises: 

 forming at least one land grid array (LGA) pad on a third surface of the interface layer, wherein the third surface of the interface layer is opposite the second surface of the interface layer; and forming at least one via in the interface layer to electrically connect the at least one contact pad with the at least on LGA pad. 19. The method of claim 18, further comprising attaching a printed circuit board (PCB) to third surface of the interface layer. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein attaching the PCB to the third surface of the interface layer comprises attaching one or more PCB contact pads to the at least one LGA pad. 21. The method of claim 19, wherein the PCB comprises a ground plane, and wherein the passive component is separated from the ground plane by a distance that is independent of a line spacing utilized by the PCB. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein a value of the passive component is dependent on the distance separating the passive component and the ground plane. 23. The method of claim 18, wherein the passive component comprises a capacitor. 24. The method of claim 18, wherein the passive component comprises an inductor. 25. The method of claim 18, wherein forming the interface layer further comprises adding a molding compound to the first surface of the glass substrate and over the passive component and the at least one contact. 26. The method of claim 18, wherein forming the at least one via in the interface layer comprises laser drilling the interface layer to expose the at least one contact pad. 27. The method of claim 18, further comprising reducing a height of the glass substrate after forming the interface layer. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein reducing the height of the glass substrate comprises grinding down a fourth surface of the glass substrate, wherein the fourth surface is opposite the first surface of the glass substrate.
